county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Albany County in 1791. Site of pivotal 1777 Battle of Saratoga. Saratoga Springs famous for mineral springs and horse racing. Saratoga National Historical Park.
county_metadata.research_tips: Saratoga County Clerk in Ballston Spa holds deed records from 1791 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1791 records in Albany County. The Battle of Saratoga (1777) was the turning point of the Revolution and Saratoga National Historical Park has military records. The Saratoga County Historical Society in Brookside Museum (Ballston Spa) maintains genealogical files and cemetery records. Saratoga Springs was a major resort from the early 1800s and hotel, racing, and mineral springs records document visitors and workers. The Saratoga Springs Public Library has local history room with city directories. For African American research, the Black community in Saratoga Springs has roots in the resort industry from the 1830s. The county's agricultural character in the eastern half means town clerk records and church records are primary sources. Mechanicville and Waterford industrial records at local historical societies.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1791; county clerk Ballston Spa

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation.
Wills, administrations from 1791.
Revolutionary War records from the Battle of Saratoga. Pension and service records for participants.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant losses beyond the 1690 massacre destruction.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Albany County in 1809. Stockade district of Schenectady is one of oldest settlements in NY. GE and ALCO headquarters. Union College. 1690 Schenectady Massacre.
county_metadata.research_tips: Schenectady County Clerk holds deed records from 1809 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1809 records in Albany County. The Schenectady County Historical Society at Mabee Farm has excellent genealogical collections including Dutch colonial family files. The 1690 Schenectady Massacre by French and Native Americans destroyed early records, but Dutch Reformed church records survive from the rebuilding period. First Reformed Church of Schenectady records (from 1694) are critical for colonial families. GE (General Electric) moved headquarters here in 1892 and employment records document thousands of workers. Union College archives (from 1795) have student records and regional manuscripts. The Stockade Historic District has some of the oldest surviving houses in the Mohawk Valley. For the Italian community, Mt. Carmel Church records (from 1903) document immigrants who worked in GE and ALCO plants.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1809; county clerk Schenectady

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Albany and Otsego Counties in 1795. Schoharie Valley was early Palatine German settlement area. Rural agricultural county. Howe Caverns tourism. Anti-rent movement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Schoharie County Clerk holds deed records from 1795 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1795 records in Albany and Otsego Counties. The Schoharie Valley was one of the earliest Palatine German settlement areas (1713+) and the Old Stone Fort Museum (Schoharie County Historical Society) has outstanding collections of German immigrant records including church registers, land patents, and family papers. The Lutheran and Reformed church records of Schoharie, Middleburgh, and Blenheim are critical for Palatine families. Revolutionary War records include the 1780 burning of Schoharie by British/Iroquois forces. The anti-rent movement affected tenant farmers here. The Gilboa Dam (1926) displaced communities and their records. Cobleskill SUNY campus has local history materials. Many Schoharie Valley families migrated to the Mohawk Valley and western NY, making these records a starting point for research in those areas.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1795; county clerk Schoharie

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Steuben, Tompkins, and Chemung Counties in 1854. Finger Lakes region. Watkins Glen gorge and racing. Seneca Lake. Small rural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Schuyler County Clerk in Watkins Glen holds deed records from 1854 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1854 records are split among Steuben, Tompkins, and Chemung Counties depending on town. This small Finger Lakes county centered on Seneca Lake has limited but well-preserved records. The Schuyler County Historical Society in Montour Falls maintains genealogical files and local histories. Watkins Glen became famous for the gorge and later for automobile racing. The Seneca Lake wine industry generated vineyard and agricultural records. Havana Glen (Montour Falls) was the original county seat. The county's connections to Tompkins (Cornell area) and Steuben counties mean cross-border research is often necessary. Salt mining at Watkins generated industrial records. The Schuyler County Historian's office has cemetery transcriptions for the county's small burying grounds.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1854; county clerk Watkins Glen

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Cayuga County in 1804. Finger Lakes region between Seneca and Cayuga Lakes. Seneca Falls was birthplace of women's rights movement (1848). Waterloo is county seat.
county_metadata.research_tips: Seneca County Clerk in Waterloo holds deed records from 1804 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1804 records in Cayuga County. Seneca Falls is the birthplace of the women's rights movement and the Women's Rights National Historical Park has records from the 1848 convention including family connections of the participants. The Seneca County Historical Society in Waterloo has genealogical files. The Seneca Falls Historical Society has records of the industrial town and its pump and knitting factories. The county's Military Tract heritage means early land patents were Revolutionary War veteran grants — check NYS Archives. Waterloo is the birthplace of Memorial Day (1866) and related records at the Waterloo Library and Historical Society. The Seneca-Cayuga Canal connected the lakes and canal records are at NYS Archives. Ovid was the original county seat and has older records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1804; county clerk Waterloo

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation. Military Tract land patents significant.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Ontario County in 1796. Southern Tier / Finger Lakes region. Named for Baron von Steuben. Corning is largest city (Corning Glass Works). Bath is county seat. Wine country.
county_metadata.research_tips: Steuben County Clerk in Bath holds deed records from 1796 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1796 records in Ontario County. The Steuben County Historical Society in Bath has genealogical files and regional records. Corning became dominant after Corning Glass Works (1868) and the Corning Museum of Glass archives document company families and workers. The county was settled by New Englanders and veterans of the Sullivan-Clinton campaign (1779) who received Military Tract grants in the area. Baron von Steuben's own estate was in the county (Steuben Memorial SHS). The wine industry along Keuka Lake generated vineyard records. Hammondsport was the birthplace of the American wine industry (Pleasant Valley Wine Company, 1860). Glenn Curtiss aviation pioneer records at Curtiss Museum in Hammondsport connect aviation families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1796; county clerk Bath

county_metadata.record_losses: Minor fire damage in the 1840s but most records survived. Overall good preservation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: One of the original 12 counties (1683). Eastern Long Island. Riverhead is county seat. Includes the Hamptons, Shelter Island, and the North and South Forks. Originally settled by New Englanders.
county_metadata.research_tips: Suffolk County Clerk in Riverhead holds deed records from 1683 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same era. Suffolk has unique dual heritage — English Puritan settlement from New England (Southold, Southampton, East Hampton from 1640) under Connecticut influence, and Dutch settlement in the west. The Suffolk County Historical Society in Riverhead has outstanding genealogical collections including family files, whaling records, and Long Island town histories. Town records are critical — many Suffolk towns predate the county and have records from the 1640s (East Hampton, Southampton, Southold). Whaling records from Sag Harbor (1760s-1870s) document maritime families. The Montauk Indian records are at the Suffolk County Historical Society and East Hampton Library. The Shinnecock Indian Nation in Southampton has tribal records. For 20th-century research, Long Island Rail Road employment records and the Grumman/Northrop aerospace records (Bethpage) document suburban-era families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1683; county clerk Riverhead

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Ulster County in 1809. Catskill Mountains region. Famous for Borscht Belt resort era. Bethel was site of 1969 Woodstock festival. Lumber and tanning industries.
county_metadata.research_tips: Sullivan County Clerk in Monticello holds deed records from 1809 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1809 records in Ulster County. The Sullivan County Historical Society in Hurleyville has genealogical files and resort-era records. The Borscht Belt (1920s-1970s) Jewish resort industry generated hotel employment records, entertainment records, and community documentation. The Delaware and Hudson Canal (1828-1898) records at the Minisink Valley Historical Society document canal workers. The county's lumber and tanning industries (1800s) generated employment records. Bethel Woods Center for the Arts archives document the Woodstock era. For earlier families, German and Scotch-Irish settlers came via the Delaware Valley. Fort Delaware in Narrowsburg has frontier-era records. The county has significant ties to NYC — many families maintained dual residences.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1809; county clerk Monticello

county_metadata.record_losses: An 1809 courthouse fire in Owego destroyed some early county records including portions of deed and court records. Records after 1809 are complete. Some early material was reconstructed from individual land owners' personal copies.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Montgomery County in 1791. Southern Tier along PA border. Owego is county seat. Originally very large; many counties carved from it. Susquehanna River valley.
county_metadata.research_tips: Tioga County Clerk in Owego holds deed records from 1809 (reconstructed after fire) and Surrogate's Court has probate from reconstruction. The 1809 courthouse fire destroyed some early records but much was reconstructed. Pre-1791 records in Montgomery County. The Tioga County Historical Society in Owego has genealogical files and the restored early records. Owego was an important Susquehanna River town and river commerce records document early families. Many early settlers came from Connecticut via the Susquehanna Company claims (disputed lands). The county was originally much larger and early records relate to areas now in Broome, Chemung, and other counties. The Coburn Free Library in Owego has local history. For PA border families, check Bradford County PA records. The Sullivan-Clinton 1779 expedition against the Iroquois passed through and military records document the route.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1809 after courthouse fire; some earlier reconstructed
